item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it. +1
One horrible thing occurred to me though. There's enough of them to make a chain around someone's torso. I wonder what horrible effect that would have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me, or does 15k$ seem pretty cheap for an anomalous device?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I find it hilarious, when you hear “BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA” and then your teeth just shoot out. I’m a sadist……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. This SCP is perfectly suited for M C & D
And I have written a tail with this SCP on Korean wiki

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upboated. Reason: Two words: SpongeBob. SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How do numbers even work

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That implies it was the following month, though, unless there's a 26th month of the year I'm unaware of. Decembuary, perhaps, or Jurgust. Either way, fix them dates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They waited until the following morning to interview him. He'd had a rough day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1808 was one of ██ SCP objects retrieved on 26/04/2004 from a Marshall, Carter & Dark, Ltd. auction house in Somerset, England, during a raid on the facility carried out by Mobile Task Force Eta-2 ("Buyer Beware").

Transcription of the post-rescue video interview with Agent Rosser, who was captured and tortured by Marshall, Carter & Dark personnel on 26/05/2004.

Which date is the right one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may get chewed out for this, but no Patrick, mayonnaise is not an SCP… No, horseradish is not an SCP either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "SCP-1808 was one of ██ SCP objects retrieved…" I feel like that sentence would be better if it were "anomalous objects" instead. After all, at the time of retrieval they wouldn't have been SCP objects yet. Even if they were, the phrase "SCP objects" sounds awkward to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took a second to realize the bones shoot oUT of the front of your fingers and toes. Thought it just elongated the fingers themselves as well.

Also.

Ow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is interesting, but the object itself is too simple. I caught myself wanting to know more, how the objects were created, why did they pick SpongeBob, and so on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is mayonnaise an SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Usually, it's just coincidental that the effect of a man-made SCP has to do with it's theme. Like, you would expect that an SCP called Spongebob watches would be like, "Oh, it makes you think you're Spongebob," or, "Oh, it makes you turn into Spongebob." I like how the effect has nothing to do with Spongebob, as that's the norm with most things; the effects being from what it would usually do, like a clock that affects time.

I give you a +1, for an interesting idea, and a well made interview log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is both absolutely horrifying and interesting all at the same time. The combination of this item's properties and the way of which it is used retains a remarkable effect. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeeeeeee this is horrible

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gets an upvote for the sheer absurdity of it all. I mean, it's a Spongebob watch that makes your fingers, toes, and teeth grow!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, it's pretty good in it's current state. Though personally I'd of curved it to make the bone growths beneficial in some sense [sharp teeth/claw like prutrusions]. Just for the sake of sheer "What the hell would you use that for". But as it is it seems like a weird, morbid and almost ironic torture device. So +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Revised the product description at the end to make MC&D sound more in-character. This is just a temporary fix until I can get around to doing a full rewrite that doesn't include any GoIs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview and the product synopsis really did it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really makes me picture MCD as some really weird corperation selling weird stuff to wierd rich people.

That's pretty much my headcanon for those guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say that the Addendum was the thing that did it for me. That last sentence. Really makes me picture MCD as some really weird corperation selling weird stuff to wierd rich people. The idea that some people in the real world would probaly enjoy this makes me shutter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. The Factory always made me think of a supernatural version of supermarket brands (As in , the one associated with the supermarket, like, well, Sainsbury for Sainsbury, or ARO for METRO or the like).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's pretty obvious that MCD didn't make these themselves, far too tacky for them to have had any creative input.
That makes me wonder about who did originate these, and what were they trying to accomplish?
The effects are horrific and painful, but non-lethal and easily reversible. I think the vital clue is the laugh track.
These watches feel to me like a textbook example of aversion conditioning. Much like the agent mentions in his interview, it wouldn't take long at all before a victim would find the very concept of spongebob intolerable.

That'll get your rugrat to change the channel, I tell you what!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Setting all else aside, these watches would be all wrong for the factory. Factory products are at least nominally utilitarian, and absolutely generic. Trademarked and copyrighted character merchandise just isn't the right style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That…got real dark real fast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it was quality assurance.

This was the best part of the entire thing, but unfortunately the rest just didn't do anything for me. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is very well argued.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't really paid attention to that line before, but now that you point it out, I see what you're saying. I'll try rewriting it in a less awkward way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To address the concerns about the GOI link:

Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd. is known for collecting rare and obscure items, along with providing its members the most exclusive, expensive, and rare experiences available.

MC&D does not sell merchandise that is necessarily glamorous, but rather items that are valuable in their uniqueness as an anomaly. They found/bought/stole the watches from someplace else and are now reselling them to the highest bidder. The watches are tacky in appearance and composed of cheap plastic, but their unique qualities are more than enough to make them a sought-after commodity among collectors.

The watches could have been from The Factory originally, but I didn't want to link them explicitly due to the simple fact that I do not like The Factory as a GOI.

Wondertainment specializes in things that can be fun and enjoyable as long as you follow the instruction manual to the letter. The SB watches aren't child friendly under any circumstances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes I forget the very very strange ways in which the Foundation's employees get tortured. This ranks up there with that guy who got killed by pasta.

The MC&D link is not that compelling. Doesn't this seem more like The Factory or Dr. Wondertainment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Marshall, Carter & Dark employee responsible for Rosser's bondage was apprehended, and with encouragement, cooperated in the removal and containment of SCP-1808.

I'm downvoting because of this sentence. It rubs me wrong in so many ways I can't even begin to elaborate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a GOI person. I feel like 99% of the time they don't add anything to an article, and most of the time they detract from it. If you are going to do it, there should probably be some sort of non-lazy thematic connection. The MC&D link is tenuous. It's an anomalous thing that causes a great deal of pain to people. I'm pretty sure those are thick on the ground in this fictional universe, and a MC&D client would probably go for something a little less tacky than a Spongebob watch.

As for the item itself, it's a little random for my tastes, and it hews too closely to the extremely basic "it's an X that does Y" formula.

Also, why on earth is the opening bid redacted?

I would downvote, but imagining the goofy Spongebob laugh followed by someone's bones shooting out of their fingertips is inexplicably funny to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The log feels slightly too .… casual , could I say? As in, it sounds like he's recounting a pub tale or something. But, I like it overall. Very nice effect, and a nice use for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mostly agreed, here. I think this is horrific & original enough to have promise, but it would be a lot more interesting as a Factory product, or something not attached to MC&D. The implications of using these as a torture device are unsettling, but the circumstances described in the log are implausible, and there are better ways to reveal the real horror of these effects than having someone talk about them. Much of it is better left to the imagination.

I'd honestly be more interested if these things were recovered "in the wild" after victims had succumbed to its effects (not necessarily lethally). The fact that these can't be removed by anyone but the person who attached them could be taken for a different angle, too— what if these instead lengthened ALL finger/toe bones, thus making it very difficult to unfasten the watch or manipulate the controls? (That might be fodder for an entirely different article, though. Don't think I've seen any SCPs based on the awkward frustration of trying to button shirtsleeves…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Common misconception.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the general idea, but the interview and the fact that you made this an MC&D item made me downvote. The log was just extremely meh and felt a bit out of tone, and this really would not be something that MC&D clients would buy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should probably be sound byte instead of sound bite. Otherwise, very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could see, as I was reading this, the uses for such a device, not to mention the ear-splitting torture of hearing Spongebob laugh every time a growth event occurred. And then the interview just went and confirmed all of that while adding in a few extra bits, and I tried to upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. The interview made this come alive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You made me fear fricking Spongebob Squarepants watches.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just don't see MC&D selling licensed memorabilia… Especially not Spongebob.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the sales copy at the end could really use some work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooooh I love when an scp gives me the shivers. It's such an adorable, cute, nice thing…. <3

+1 with absolute glee

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pat on the back award sounds entirely too casual. I get that that's the idea, but it's still mildly distracting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BAHAHAHAHAHAHA

BAHAHAHAHAHAHA

BAHAHAHAHAHAHA

BAHAHAHAHAHAHA

Spongebob makes this one better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly don't know whether I love this or hate this. I think I'll settle on both.

No-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even know what to think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
O_O

At first, when I read the words "Spongebob Squarepants" I didn't think this was going to be noteworthy.
But that interview log changed my mind.
Damn, this thing is creepy.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My third SCP, submitted for your approval.

Discover more of my weird ramblings in
~PeppersGhost's Gallery of Illusion!~
